raw_consumer_statement:
Dear representative, I was scammed by XXXX XXXX. 
Found concert tickets on the XXXX website ( p. XXXX ), ordered tickets, and got a confirmation ( p XXXX ) : Youll receive an email with instructions and tickets from the original provider, not XXXX. Found tickets 2 times cheaper on XXXX XXXX. Tried to cancel the XXXX order, no cancellation only a sale on the same website. Tried to sell, but nothing to sell, tried to find from them where are the tickets and who is tickets provider, no result. A charge of {$830.00} appeared on my Citibank statement. 

Mailed dispute forms may to Citibank Government Services. Dispute forms were delivered on XX/XX/2023. 
Tracking # XXXX XXXX created a double scam ( p. XXXX ) : Send your tickets for sale, if you do not send charges will apply. 
A charge of {$300.00} appeared on my Citibank statement. 

Mailed dispute forms may to Citibank Government Services. Dispute forms were delivered on XXXX XX/XX/2023 Tracking # XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX According to FTC regulation Within 30 days of getting your complaint, the issuer must acknowledge it in writing, unless the problem has been resolved.. 

https : //consumer.ftc.gov/articles/using-credit-cards-and-disputing-charges But the charge is still on my account. Please please help to correct this issue. 
Thank you.
